itinerary.route: Berg Lake TH to Kinney Lake
itinerary.mapNote: Flat valley walk beside the Robson River. Kinney Lake turquoise water backed by Mount Robson's lower walls. 1050m.

itinerary.route: Kinney Lake to Berg Lake via Valley of a Thousand Falls
itinerary.mapNote: Emperor Falls at 67m, Robson Falls, and Whitehorn Falls — three major cascades in quick succession. Berg Lake arrival. 1646m.

itinerary.route: Berg Lake day exploration — Hargreaves Glacier Meadows
itinerary.mapNote: Hargreaves Meadows above the lake — full view of Mount Robson and the Berg Glacier calving into the lake. 2100m.

itinerary.route: Berg Lake to Trailhead — full return
itinerary.mapNote: Descent past all falls again in reverse. Mount Robson's summit often revealed on morning return. 856m TH.

waypoints.name: Mount Robson Summit
waypoints.note: Highest peak in the Canadian Rockies — often cloud-capped. A clear sighting is rare and celebrated.

waypoints.name: Emperor Falls
waypoints.note: 67-metre waterfall — the trail passes directly beside the cascade on a narrow ledge trail.

waypoints.name: Berg Lake
waypoints.note: Turquoise glacial lake with icebergs calved from the Berg Glacier. Mount Robson rises directly above.

waypoints.name: Berg Glacier
waypoints.note: Active glacier calving directly into Berg Lake — watch for iceberg falls from the camp or meadows.

waypoints.name: Hargreaves Glacier Meadows
waypoints.note: The finest viewpoint on the trail — Mount Robson, Berg Glacier, and the lake all visible simultaneously.

waypoints.name: Kinney Lake
waypoints.note: Turquoise lake at the foot of Mount Robson's approach walls — striking on arrival and departure.
